item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, same.

I really want to like this, but my attempts to put the pieces together just feel like I'm grasping at straws.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… yeah… I have to no vote because this one just isn't making sense to me at all. I must be missing the twist here. I dont understand why people were afraid, I don't get the singing… I don't get why the live passenger is allowed on board. I'm just not seeing the connections here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Similarly, inform/announce in: "An SCP-4927-3 instance will always inform that SCP-4927-1 has made a stop in a flight towards notable population centers."

I liked this, but I definitely feel like I'm missing something. I get that its a ghost plane, but (for instance) why are most people on it scared while the D-class is saying its like heaven? no vote based on what I can see now, might change to an upvote if what I'm not seeing finally hits me.

Edited to add...
– hide block

I doubt it's coincidence that the date range just happens to coincide with the Cold War, but I don't see a connection.

Wait… is the twist that the passengers die by suicide? "I'm not man enough…", suicide during an acid induced freakout… "I'm doing this for my children" = common misguided thinking of the suicidal… and checking back, the father that was seen died in an aviation incident, not an aviation accident, which is how I thought I remembered it.

this is growing on me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short and sweet, I love this one for what (I think) was the conclusion based on what the mother stated.

One minor nitpick.

D-4657 told that he saw a SCP-4927-2 instance resembling his father.

Told who?

http://www.learnersdictionary.com/qa/when-do-you-use-said-and-told

When using the past tense of tell, you need to point towards someone. X tells Y. X told Y.

With said, the receiver isn't needed as its just what X is saying at that moment.

item.title: small mistake
item.content:encoded:
Hi, have to admit I don't fully get the twist, but it was a nice read anyway.

I did notice a small mistake it think, in the fifth paragraph of the description it says:

When SCP-4927-1 instances, along with the affected subject, board SCP-4927-1, the gate will close from the inside and then SCP-4927-1 will take off,

Shouldn't it be "when SCP-4927-2 instances, along…" it seems kind of wierd for the plane to board the plane doesn't it? I thought I'd check with the author first so I don't change something without permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read it three times, and I still feel like I'm missing something. What I'm getting is that the plane is full of dead people (died in airplane accidents?). Is there anything else?

item.title: WOAH
item.content:encoded:
At first I was going to novote, but then the twist slapped me across the face in the best possible way.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No. But you just have to read carefully.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i dont know what im missing. am i dumb?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It took me a bit longer to understand than I want to admit.
+1

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Dc_Yerko and RockTeethMothEyes for giving me feedback.

Enjoy this skip!!
